rationale: The citing paper uses Bronnenberg, Mahajan, and Vanhonacker (2000) in core theory-building for its distribution section, specifically to justify the expected positive effect of distribution breadth on base sales. It also cites that study when motivating dynamic dependencies among marketing variables through sales and when defining ACV-weighted distribution in the variable operationalization. These are not just bibliography-list mentions; the cited work informs both conceptual framing and measurement choices in the citing model.
relation_description: The citing paper treats the cited study as prior foundation for how distribution breadth and sales are linked and for handling distribution-related dynamics in empirical modeling. It builds its mature-brand, full-mix analysis on that prior distribution logic rather than directly testing the cited paper's new-category market-structure model.
